title: Plutarchia coronaria
intro: Plutarchia coronaria, also known as the Crown-of-Thorns, is a flowering plant species within the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Plutarchia coronaria include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions such as acne and eczema. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Plutarchia coronaria include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin irritations and promote wound healing.
